"'''Aach'''" was the code name of a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served as a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] agent during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="Interlude" /> In [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], he informed [[Garm Bel Iblis/Legends|Garm Bel Iblis]] of the [[Death Star plans/Legends|Death Star plans]]. Directly answerable to [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Organa]], Aach was a special messenger that was only used for top-level Alliance business.<ref name="Interlude" />

Though he was willing to forgo his return to [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] and accompany Bel Iblis on his mission to [[Darkknell/Legends|Darkknell]], the [[Senator/Legends|senator]] sent him back to Organa with firm belief that Aach would be needed elsewhere.<ref name="Interlude" />

==Behind the scenes==
Aach appeared in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] novella "[[Interlude at Darkknell]]" by [[Timothy Zahn]] and [[Michael A. Stackpole]], which was published in the [[1999]] anthology ''[[Tales from the New Republic]]''.<ref name="Interlude" /> After the [[2014]] reboot of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' continuity,<ref name="Canon">{{SW|url=news/the-legendary-star-wars-expanded-universe-turns-a-new-page|text=The Legendary Star Wars Expanded Universe Turns a New Page}}</ref> the [[2016]] [[canon]] reference book ''[[Star Wars: Rogue One: The Ultimate Visual Guide]]'', a tie-in to the film ''[[Rogue One: A Star Wars Story]]'', established that [[Cassian Jeron Andor]], one of the film's protagonists, had used the code name "Aach" as a senatorial contact on [[Darkknell]].<ref name="R1 VG">''[[Star Wars: Rogue One: The Ultimate Visual Guide]]''</ref>
